Made by Multitudes
Index Info Memos
WED, MAY 13, 2026

Internal — Not Indexed

Style Guide

The visual system for madebymultitudes.com. Use this page to keep new components and content consistent with the rest of the site. The page itself is not linked from anywhere and is excluded from search engines.

Tokens — Colour

All colour comes from CSS custom properties on :root. Use the token, not the hex, in new CSS.

TokenHexSampleRole
--paper #f4f0e8 Background. Warm cream paper.
--paper-deep #ece6da Background, slightly deeper. Section breaks, hover fills.
--ink #161513 Body text, headings. The darkest ink.
--ink-soft #4a463f Secondary text, footer prose, active link state.
--ink-mute #8a857a Tertiary text, metadata labels, inactive states.
--rule #c9c2b3 Hairline dividers, section separators.
--accent #2a3f6e Deep ink-blue. Hover states. Used sparingly.

Tokens — Type

Three primary faces. Each has one job. Don't mix display roles (e.g. don't set body text in Momo Trust Display).

TokenFaceRole
--display Momo Trust Display Display headlines. The hero "made by multitudes", page titles. Lowercase by default at display sizes; mixed case elsewhere. Single weight (400).
--body Manrope Body text, blurbs, intro paragraphs, about-page copy. Variable weight 300–800; default 400.
--post Crimson Pro Legacy serif. Available as an alternate post-body style. Currently unused on memo posts (those use Courier Prime).
--mono JetBrains Mono Chrome: nav, wordmark, section labels, metadata, URLs, footer readout. Weights 400 and 500.

Specimens

Display — Momo Trust Display, lowercase

made by multitudes

Body — Manrope, 17px / 1.55

We create products, services and brands that focus on learning and growth. The body is set in Manrope, a humanist sans with generous proportions.

Mono — JetBrains Mono, uppercase, tracked

Operating globally · Based on Wadawurrung Country · Active

Tokens — Size & Spacing

TokenValueRole
--t-xs 0.72rem Micro labels, mono captions, metadata.
--t-sm 0.84rem Small body, blurbs, attribution lines.
--t-base 1.0625rem Default body size.
--t-md 1.25rem Lead paragraphs, mid-size headings.
--t-lg 1.875rem Index titles, section headings.
--t-xl 2.75rem Page-level h1 (non-display).
--t-display clamp(2.6rem, 6vw, 4.4rem) Hero display. Fluid based on viewport.
--measure 64ch Maximum reading line length for body prose.
--gutter clamp(1.25rem, 4vw, 3rem) Horizontal page padding. Fluid.

Pattern — Index Row

The core layout used on the homepage (ventures, projects) and the memo index. Three columns: est. date left, title + blurb + url middle, status right.

  • est. 2026 Example Venture A one-sentence description of what the venture does. Plain prose, no marketing voice. example.com Active

On mobile (below 720px), the row collapses to two columns and the status indicator moves underneath the title.

Pattern — Status

Each venture or project has one of four statuses. The status is set via a class on the right-hand element of an index row. Add new statuses sparingly — the value of a closed system is its meaning being legible at a glance.

StatusClassColourUse for
Active .status-active #4a7d4a Currently operating ventures.
Acquired .status-acquired #2a3f6e Ventures sold or merged.
For Sale .status-for-sale #b8842d Available for acquisition.
Closed .status-closed #8a857a Wound down. Kept on the list for transparency.

Pattern — Links

Standard prose links inherit the ink colour and underline subtly. On hover they shift to --accent.

Here is a paragraph with an example link embedded in it. Hover the link to see the colour shift.

Navigation links are uppercase mono and use the active-state marker aria-current="page", which is styled with a leading · marker. See the site header above for an active example.

Component — Memo Hero (Posts Only)

The memo header treatment is scoped exclusively to memo posts. It is not used elsewhere on the site. Modeled on a declassified government memorandum: a corner form number, a banner with the studio name, an italic "Memorandum" showpiece, and four bureaucratic fields (TO, FROM, SUBJECT, DATE).

  • Form number is derived from the post's chronological position. Oldest published = FORM NO. 01.
  • TO is the to: frontmatter field (defaults to "All").
  • FROM is the author: frontmatter field (defaults to "Matt Smith").
  • SUBJECT is the post title, underlined for emphasis.
  • DATE is the publish date in DD/MM/YY.

Post bodies inside the memo wrapper render in Courier Prime with Special Elite section headings — the typewriter treatment. This is the only place on the site where these typefaces appear.

Component — Footer Readout

A single justified mono sentence containing the studio name, location (linked to /acknowledge), live date/time, and current weather. The location, date/time and weather are not bold; emphasis comes from a darker ink colour (--ink-soft) against the surrounding --ink-mute prose.

Weather is fetched client-side from Open-Meteo (CC BY 4.0). The studio location, latitude/longitude, and timezone are configured in src/layouts/Base.astro via the STUDIO constant.

Voice

  • Plain. Specific. Short where possible. Drop marketing words: solutions, leverage, innovative, journey. Use the actual thing instead.
  • "We" not "I", except in memos. The studio is the first-person plural. Memo bylines (the author: frontmatter) can be first-person singular.
  • No emoji. No exclamation marks. One acceptable exception: the working principles use commands ("Be competent and committed.") with full stops, not bangs.
  • Em-dashes are fine, generously. Use a real em-dash (—), not a hyphen.
  • Wadawurrung Country when referring to location. Not Ballarat.

Routes

PathFilePurpose
/pages/index.astroHomepage. Hero + ventures index + (hidden) projects.
/aboutpages/about.astroLong-form. Linked from nav as "Info".
/memopages/memo/index.astroMemo index. Posts grouped by year.
/memo/[slug]pages/memo/[...slug].astroIndividual memo. Uses memo hero treatment.
/acknowledgepages/acknowledge.astroAcknowledgement of Country.
/stylepages/style.astroThis page. noindex + blocked in robots.txt.

Made by Multitudes · Operating globally, based on Wadawurrung Country where it is currently Wed, 13 May 2026.

© 2026 Weather · Open-Meteo
